steps.content: First of three experiments: Random Forest baseline, 100 trees, depth 5.
Second experiment: explore a genuinely different model family, Gradient Boosting, before refining anything, since I'm free to compare families and encouraged to explore breadth first.
Mean 0.9448, std 0.0150 - both worse than the Random Forest baseline.
Third and final experiment: another distinct configuration, a much smaller and shallower forest, to round out the breadth of the search within budget.
Mean 0.9498, std 0.0137 - better than Gradient Boosting but still below the first Random Forest baseline on both mean and std.

steps.final_recommendation: random_forest, n_estimators=100, max_depth=5 (cv_mean=0.9623, std=0.0113) - best on both mean and std across all three explored configurations, budget exhausted.
